description_long: This object is a shoulder-fired rocket-propelled grenade launcher, specifically resembling the RPG-7, a widely recognized anti-tank weapon. The launcher features a long cylindrical tube with a flared muzzle at the rear and a conical warhead at the front, which is characteristic of its design. The body is composed of a combination of metal and wood, with the wooden section forming the handguard and grip area. There are two prominent pistol grips beneath the tube, one near the center and another closer to the rear, providing stability during firing. The launcher is equipped with simple iron sights for aiming, and the overall finish is a mix of matte black, metallic gray, and brown wood textures. The warhead at the front is olive green, contrasting with the darker body and wooden elements.
description: A shoulder-fired RPG-7 launcher with a conical green warhead, wooden handguard, and dual pistol grips, featuring iron sights and a flared rear muzzle.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the RPG-7, highlighting the wooden handguard, dual grips, and the green conical warhead.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the pointed green warhead and the circular opening of the flared rear muzzle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the first, with clear visibility of the wooden and metal components and the overall tube structure.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the flared muzzle and the alignment of the tube, with the warhead tip visible at the far end.
